title: Kernel orthestrationについて、codexだけでなくglmやclaudeとの協奏として設定されているか確認せよ
summary_text: - **Quota brownout cascade:** Claude/GLM hit 429/usage caps, retries consume the remaining budget, and orchestration either hard-blocks (availability loss) or silently degrades if any policy toggle is loosened. - **Asymmetric quota failure:** one diversity provider is rate-limited while Codex is healthy, creating pressure to accept Codex-only continuation; that is a multi-agent reality break if admitted. - **Stale session contamination:** old rollout/session artifacts are mistaken for current-lane evidence, so checks pass while current run has insufficient live lanes. - **Stale provider evidence window:** recent-model logs from another task/session satisfy “required providers observed,” masking missing Claude/GLM in the active run. - **Upstream slash path bypass:** bare `/kernel` behavior in Codex upstream changes (or is parsed as plain text), bypassing local launcher/guard path and losing enforced bootstrap guarantees. - **Prompt drift across repos:** `fugue-orchestrator` and `feed-runner` contract/test scripts diverge, so one repo allows weaker startup semantics than the other. - **Local/global prompt precedence drift:** repo-local and `~/.codex/prompts` mismatch causes operators to run with stale wording despite passing familiar commands. - **Lane evidence forgery-by-format:** lane manifest text appears valid, but lanes are planned/pending/failed; evidence parser accepts shape without strong liveness proof. - **Provider check false-ready:** static readiness (`binary/key present`) passes even when runtime provider calls fail (model drift, auth scope, endpoint issues), collapsing true diversity. - **Long-running daemon state rot:** watchdog/monitor loops survive restarts with stale PID/session assumptions, counting dead lanes as active or missing newly spawned lanes. - **Clock/window skew bugs:** time-based freshness windows (for sessions/evidence) fail under clock drift/sleep-wake, causing false pass or false fail on multi-agent compliance. - **Backpressure backlog collapse:** queue growth under prolonged degradation causes delayed lane starts; by the time analysis begins, effective concurrency is below policy minimum.
